schemas.name: Income Tax Brackets Comparison 2026
schemas.description: Compare income tax brackets and top marginal rates across 40+ countries for the 2026 tax year.

schemas.headline: Income Tax Brackets by Country 2026
schemas.description: Definitive comparison of income tax rates, VAT, and tax brackets for 40+ countries in 2026. See how the US compares to Canada, Europe, and globally.

mainEntity.name: Which country has the highest income tax?

acceptedAnswer.text: Based on our 2026 data, countries like Sweden, Denmark, and Austria frequently rank at the top with highest marginal income tax rates approaching or exceeding 50-55%.

mainEntity.name: What are the US federal tax brackets for 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: For 2026, barring new legislation extending the TCJA, the US federal tax brackets are scheduled to revert to their pre-2018 structures. The top marginal rate is expected to return to 39.6% from 37%.

mainEntity.name: Do any countries have 0% income tax?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, several countries have a 0% personal income tax rate, including the United Arab Emirates (UAE) and Saudi Arabia, although they may levy other taxes like VAT or corporate taxes.

mainEntity.name: How does Canada's tax rate compare to the US?

acceptedAnswer.text: Canada generally has higher top marginal tax rates compared to the US, often exceeding 50% when combining federal and provincial taxes, whereas the US top combined rate is typically lower, though this varies significantly by state/province.

mainEntity.name: What is the average tax rate in Europe?

acceptedAnswer.text: The top marginal tax rate in Western Europe typically averages around 40-50%, often accompanied by substantial VAT rates (around 20%) and social security contributions.
